fix: replace variable bind mounts with hardcoded paths/named volumes

Coolify converts ${VAR}:/path bind mounts to named Docker volumes when
the variable is not resolved. Fixed per stack:

- adguard: -> named volumes (data already in Coolify-created volumes)
- authentik: -> named volumes (data already in Coolify-created volumes)
- gitea: -> /opt/gitea/{postgres,data,runner}
- mail-relay: -> /opt/mail-relay/{queue,opendkim,secrets/...}
- media-server: COMMON_PATH -> /opt/media (hardcoded)
- trilium: -> /opt/trilium/data
- wireguard: -> /opt/wg-easy + /lib/modules

Add transaction history blocks to NFC card: anti-tamper checksum, atomic writes, last-4 display (#23)

* Initial plan

* Implement transaction history data blocks for NFC card

- Add TransactionBlock.kt: new data class with 32-byte layout (2 blocks)
  spanning initTimestamp (4B), up to 4 tx entries (6B each: 3B seconds
  offset, 1B op, 2B amount), and 4-byte HMAC-SHA256 checksum keyed with
  PSK+UID for anti-tamper detection.
- Update MainActivity to read/write all 3 data blocks; verify checksum
  before every add/deduct (refuse with error on tamper); retry interrupted
  writes using in-memory PendingWrite state; initialize transaction blocks
  with timestamp on format; display last 4 transactions after card read.
- Add transaction history section to activity_main.xml (gone by default).
- Add new string resources for transaction display and error messages.

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/f5b9270f-88fe-4b32-9a2e-f25468f92a31

* Convert balance counter to Mifare Classic Value Block format

- Add Byte.inv8() helper (xor 0xFF) since Kotlin's Byte.inv() is Int
- Add makeValueBlock(value): builds 16-byte Mifare Classic Value Block:
  value(4B LE) | ~value(4B) | value(4B) | addr ~addr addr ~addr
- Add readValueBlock(data): parses and validates triple-redundancy,
  returns null if block is not a valid Value Block
- readAndShowBalance: parse balance with readValueBlock()
- readAndDeduct: parse with readValueBlock(), build checksum with
  makeValueBlock(newBalance), use mifare.decrement()+transfer()
  (atomic chip-level operation) instead of writeBlock()
- addBalanceToCard: same — readValueBlock() + increment()+transfer()
- formatCard (both paths): initialise counter with makeValueBlock(0)
  instead of ByteArray(16) (all zeros)
- Recovery (PendingWrite): writeBlock() with full Value Block bytes
- Add MAX_SECONDS_OFFSET constant to TransactionBlock (24-bit limit)
- Update class-level KDoc to describe Value Block format

* Add "Verificar integridad de tarjeta" setting (enabled by default)

When disabled, invalid checksums in write operations (readAndDeduct,
addBalanceToCard) are ignored and the transaction proceeds normally.
The written blocks always get a fresh valid checksum via toBytes().
Interrupted-write retry logic is unaffected (runs before the setting check).
Read-only path (readAndShowBalance) still shows red background regardless.

feat: record a status report

I added Tuist as a development tool. Tuist allows me to build the Apple
application configuration using Swift and then generate Xcode projects
and the workspace from that configuration. This is a much more
maintainable approach than manually maintaining Xcode project files, and
it also allows me to generate the Xcode project files on demand, which
is helpful for keeping the project files clean and avoiding merge
conflicts.

I updated the software requirements document to include mise-en-place,
Xcodes.app, and Xcode.

I created RecordingSession, an NSObject subclass conforming to
AVCaptureFileOutputRecordingDelegate, which manages AVCaptureSession
lifecycle on a dedicated serial DispatchQueue and handles partial
recording preservation via AVErrorRecordingSuccessfullyFinishedKey.

I created CameraPreview, a UIViewRepresentable that hosts a UIView
whose layerClass is AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer, providing an
efficient GPU-composited camera preview for SwiftUI.

I created RecordingViewModel, an @Observable @MainActor class that
manages recording state transitions, a countdown timer using Swift
structured concurrency, permission checking with AVCaptureDevice,
and VoiceOver announcements at the 10s and 5s thresholds.

I created CountdownTimerView, a circular progress gauge that
colour-codes urgency (yellow at 10s, red at 5s) with a companion
text label for WCAG 1.4.1 compliance.

I created RecordingViewModelTests and CountdownTimerTests using
Swift Testing to cover state transitions, timer countdown,
urgency boundary transitions, re-record, and early stop.

I created RecordingScreen, which composes the camera preview,
countdown timer overlay, record/stop controls, permission denied
guidance, and an error banner. It navigates to ReviewScreen
when recording completes.

I created VideoPlayerView, a thin AVKit VideoPlayer wrapper that
creates an AVPlayer from a local file URL.

I created ReviewScreen, which plays back the recorded video and
provides a Re-record button with a discard confirmation alert.
Navigating back via the iOS back gesture also cleans up the
finished recording.

I updated MainView to add a NavigationStack with a list entry
linking to RecordingScreen.

I created the architecture decision record documenting the choice
to use AVFoundation directly over UIImagePickerController, the
RecordingSessionProtocol abstraction, the concurrency model using
nonisolated(unsafe) with a dedicated session DispatchQueue, partial
recording preservation via AVErrorRecordingSuccessfullyFinishedKey,
the UIViewRepresentable camera preview bridge, and the @Observable
@MainActor ViewModel pattern.

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: sprint intervals never parsed from Intervals.icu API

Root cause: /intervals endpoint returns { icu_intervals: [...] } (an object),
not a bare array. The code checked Array.isArray(raw) which always failed,
causing every activity to silently return 0 intervals — hence 'No interval
history — using Vmax model only' on every session.

Three fixes applied:

1. useIntervalsData.ts — read raw.icu_intervals instead of raw
   (falls back to raw itself if the API ever returns a bare array)

2. parser.ts — filter rest periods that Intervals.icu labels WORK
   Intervals.icu sometimes records the walk-back recovery between sprint
   reps as a WORK segment (e.g. 300 s, 63 m, avg 0.21 m/s). These have a
   high max_speed from the preceding sprint but very low average_speed.
   New MIN_SPRINT_AVG_SPEED = 4.0 m/s check rejects them.

3. parser.ts + schema.ts — accept RECOVERY/INTERVAL/LAP interval types
   Switched from an allowlist (WORK, ACTIVE only) to a denylist
   (REST, ACTIVE_REST, WARMUP, COOLDOWN, RECOVERY). This lets auto-detected
   efforts (type INTERVAL or LAP) through to the distance/speed filter.

4. schema.ts — add training_load field
   The actual API field is training_load not icu_training_load.

Tests: 6 new parser tests added (RECOVERY exclusion, INTERVAL/LAP inclusion,
low-average-speed WORK rejection, no-average_speed pass-through).
All 18 test files pass (459 tests total).

commit.message:
feat: add 12-week periodized sprint training plan

Implements a full 12-week training plan for 100m–400m sprint development,
anchored to upcoming race events from Intervals.icu. The plan auto-detects
which week the athlete is in and generates an NFI-adjusted workout for today.

Phases:
- Weeks 1–4: GPP — acceleration mechanics, tempo conditioning
- Weeks 5–8: SPP — speed endurance, special endurance (200–300 m)
- Weeks 9–10: Pre-Competition — race-specific speed, sharpening
- Weeks 11–12: Competition — taper, volume reduction, race prep

New files:
- src/domain/sprint/training-plan.ts — 12-week plan spec + workout generators
- src/components/TrainingPlanPanel.tsx — UI: week progress, today's session
- tests/domain/sprint/training-plan.test.ts — 29 tests (all passing)

Modified files:
- src/hooks/useIntervalsData.ts — adds trainingPlan to IntervalsDataState
- src/components/Dashboard.tsx — renders TrainingPlanPanel when plan active
- src/App.tsx — passes trainingPlan prop to Dashboard

NFI integration:
- Green NFI → full prescribed session
- Amber NFI → volume reduced ~25 %
- Red + negative TSB (fatigued) → converted to recovery session
- Red + positive TSB (stale) → kept with re-activation note

All 454 tests passing. TypeScript clean.

fix: align pie chart drawing-model interpolation with Cartesian pattern

- Extract PieChartDrawingModel and interpolation logic into dedicated
  files, with SliceInfo as a nested class
- Add PieChartDrawingModelInterpolator interface and default
  implementation, matching the Cartesian layer interpolator pattern
- Restructure PieChartHost to pre-compute drawing models during
  animation (setModels on model change, transform during animation)
  instead of inline in the Canvas draw block
- Use the current interpolated state as the "old" model in setModels
  for smooth mid-animation transitions (matching views behavior)
- Make compose transform non-suspend (compose animate callback isn't
  a suspend context; pie interpolation is trivial unlike Cartesian)
- Iterate over model entries in draw/getCircleBounds (not drawing
  model slices), with drawing model as optional override and raw-data
  fallback, matching how Cartesian layers handle removed entries
- Remove hostExtraStore.clear() in views PieChartModelProducer to
  preserve the old drawing model for diffing (was causing every
  update to animate from scratch instead of interpolating)

commit.message:
Initial commit: pupsourcing/store event store module

Extracted from pupsourcing/core as a standalone event store module with:

- Core types: Event, PersistedEvent, Stream, AppendResult, ExpectedVersion
- Store interfaces: EventStore, EventReader, GlobalPositionReader, AggregateStreamReader
- Consumer interfaces: Consumer, ScopedConsumer
- PostgreSQL implementation with optimistic concurrency and NOTIFY support
- SQL migration generator (events + aggregate_heads tables)
- Event mapping code generator (eventmap-gen)
- Integration tests for PostgreSQL
- Examples: basic, cockroachdb-basic, eventmap-codegen
- CI workflow, linter config, Makefile
- GitHub Copilot agents and instructions

Key design decisions:
- All store methods accept *sql.Tx (no DBTX interface)
- Flattened package layout (store/ is the root package)
- No bounded_context concept
- PostgreSQL-only (no MySQL)

commit.message:
Add W3C Trace Context propagation for OpenTelemetry (#57)

Implement bidirectional W3C Trace Context propagation between the
Laravel SDK and Copilot CLI, mirroring the official Python/Node.js SDKs.

Outbound (SDK → CLI):
- Inject traceparent/tracestate into session.create, session.resume,
  and session.send RPC params via TraceContext::get()

Inbound (CLI → SDK):
- Restore OpenTelemetry context in tool handlers via
  TraceContext::restore()/detach()

New files:
- src/Support/TraceContext.php - W3C Trace Context helpers
- tests/Unit/Support/TraceContextTest.php
- docs/jp/telemetry.md

Changes:
- config/copilot.php: Add telemetry configuration option
- composer.json: Add open-telemetry/api to suggest
